title: Pranks And Greens

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening of episode
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but not original at this point in the series

jokes.text: Twelve Days of Christmas parody with Springfield characters
jokes.setup: Traditional Christmas carol structure
jokes.punchline: Springfield residents replacing traditional gifts - 'Twelve Grampas grumbling, Eleven Barneys belching'
jokes.explanation: Clever parody that uses established character traits effectively, good structure following the original song

jokes.text: Two special Ralphs
jokes.setup: Continuing the parody
jokes.punchline: Ralph Wiggum described as 'special' in both senses
jokes.explanation: Good character reference but relies somewhat on cheap disability humor

jokes.text: That is a rebuilt Yugoslavian engine. There isn't even a Yugoslavia anymore!
jokes.setup: Skinner identifying Bart's punishment work
jokes.punchline: Historical/political reference about a defunct country
jokes.explanation: Smart political humor that works on multiple levels - both absurdist and observational

jokes.text: Rent? Rent to own. Just 35 more payments, and it's halfway mine.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning Skinner about renting a car from his mother
jokes.punchline: Skinner's pathetic financial situation revealed
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - perfectly captures Skinner's pathetic relationship with his mother and financial incompetence

jokes.text: He? It's a she?! Just kidding. It's a guy.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about the mysterious prankster
jokes.punchline: Skinner's fake-out about the gender
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke, competently executed but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Wow! Imagine his sidekick.
jokes.setup: Bart telling Milhouse about the legendary prankster
jokes.punchline: Milhouse immediately thinking about being the sidekick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - he immediately identifies with being the sidekick rather than the hero

jokes.text: Man, if he lost that giant inhaler he'd really be in trouble with his parents.
jokes.setup: Milhouse looking at what appears to be a fire extinguisher
jokes.punchline: Mistaking it for an oversized inhaler
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag that plays on Milhouse's asthma and innocent misperception

jokes.text: Poop in the tub / I'm not gonna poop in the tub
jokes.setup: Educational children's song about bath safety
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and gross warning about defecating
jokes.explanation: Absurdist take on children's educational programming - original but somewhat cheap shock humor

jokes.text: Hydroxeos?! Marge, how could you?!
jokes.setup: Mother seeing processed cookies at snack time
jokes.punchline: Treating Oreos like they're toxic waste
jokes.explanation: Good satire of helicopter parenting and health food obsession

jokes.text: I would sooner pour the polluted water of the Ganges down their gullets
jokes.setup: Mother's horror at processed food
jokes.punchline: Dramatically preferring polluted river water to milk
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and absurdist comparison, very quotable

jokes.text: Look at Skinner... And here he is after the gap
jokes.setup: Examining school newspaper photos
jokes.punchline: Visual transformation from cool to uptight Skinner
jokes.explanation: Strong visual storytelling that sets up the main plot mystery effectively

jokes.text: A prank that changed Skinner from cool to tool
jokes.setup: Bart theorizing about what happened to Skinner
jokes.punchline: Cool to tool rhyme
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyme joke - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I haven't changed! I've always been Swim Teacher Willie and I always will be!
jokes.setup: Willie denying he's changed
jokes.punchline: Claiming he's always been a swim teacher when clearly he's now a groundskeeper
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Willie's denial and psychological damage

jokes.text: Well, I'd be a pretty lousy brother if I didn't.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Bart to distribute newspapers
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic acknowledgment that he's normally a lousy brother
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware character moment for Bart

jokes.text: I shaved and waxed the boys myself
jokes.setup: Willie describing his coaching duties
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately intimate grooming of students
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's somewhat uncomfortable and relies on inappropriate implications

jokes.text: Yo, Skin-Man. Little help? / Right back at you, Jason. Stay in school. I will!
jokes.setup: Flashback to cool young Skinner
jokes.punchline: Skinner being hip and encouraging, student already in school
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast showing how different Skinner used to be, with mild irony about staying in school

jokes.text: Worms!
jokes.setup: Skinner jumping into what he thinks is a pool
jokes.punchline: Discovering the pool is full of worms
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff for the central prank, visceral and memorable

jokes.text: Shut this pool down, Groundskeeper Willie
jokes.setup: Traumatized Skinner after the worm incident
jokes.punchline: Willie becomes groundskeeper instead of swim coach
jokes.explanation: Ties together the origin story explaining Willie's current job and Skinner's transformation

jokes.text: Never tell me where you learned his name. Or I'll kill whoever told you.
jokes.setup: Willie revealing Andy Hamilton's name
jokes.punchline: Threatening to kill himself if Bart tells anyone Willie told him
jokes.explanation: Clever logical paradox delivered in Willie's characteristic violent style

jokes.text: Don't worry, my sweets! I'll put out the fire! / That topping is a petroleum by-product!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to save burning junk food
jokes.punchline: Using flammable whipped cream to put out fire
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer physical comedy with chemical absurdity thrown in

jokes.text: I once followed Santa home from the department store and what I saw wasn't pretty.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning Bart about meeting heroes
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about department store Santa's real life
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about childhood disillusionment with excellent economy

jokes.text: Crazy Glue! / Eh, needs a topper. / Look, somebody had left glue loosener just lying around!
jokes.setup: Bart gets pranked with crazy glue
jokes.punchline: Andy casually providing the solution, showing his expertise
jokes.explanation: Well-structured prank sequence that establishes Andy's skill and casualness about elaborate setups

jokes.text: It's a real snake... It's defanged. Although he can still rub poison on you with his gums.
jokes.setup: Andy offering potato chips to Bart
jokes.punchline: Snake in can, then escalating danger with poisonous gums
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from classic prank to genuinely dangerous, with specific absurd detail about gum poison

jokes.text: It's a real snake. Don't worry. It's defanged. Although he can still rub poison on you with his gums.
jokes.setup: Andy offering what appears to be a normal snack
jokes.punchline: Escalating danger levels with casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with false reassurance followed by new danger

jokes.text: Some chick missed her own wedding!
jokes.setup: Andy describing his elaborate plane prank
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of someone's life being ruined
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Andy's sociopathic lack of empathy for prank consequences

jokes.text: How could someone so much like me be a loser?
jokes.setup: Bart realizing Andy might be a loser
jokes.punchline: His identification with Andy making him question himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bart's self-awareness and concern about his future

jokes.text: Dad, Lisa's making me see things from both sides again!
jokes.setup: Lisa presenting logical arguments to Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart treating critical thinking as an attack
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Lisa's role as voice of reason and how annoying that can be to others

jokes.text: And when his mom gets home from work she makes him whatever sandwich he wants.
jokes.setup: Bart admiring Andy's 'perfect life'
jokes.punchline: The revelation that it's just a man-child living with his mother
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reveal about what Bart sees as enviable vs. pathetic reality

jokes.text: I don't see any standees for chips, standees for soda. I don't see any standees at all!
jokes.setup: Homer entering health food store
jokes.punchline: His specific obsession with promotional standees as markers of proper stores
jokes.explanation: Great Homer observation about consumer culture and his specific fixation on promotional displays

jokes.text: This store doesn't use plastic bags because they end up in the ocean and upset jellyfish mating rituals.
jokes.setup: Explaining environmental consciousness
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific concern about jellyfish sexuality
jokes.explanation: Funny environmental concern with absurd specificity about sea life

jokes.text: Stupid horny jellyfish, neutering our dudes
jokes.setup: Learning about plastic bags affecting jellyfish mating
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude interpretation and blame assignment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - blaming jellyfish for environmental problems while using crude language

jokes.text: $830 for health food / Without preservatives, this food won't last a day
jokes.setup: Expensive health food purchase
jokes.punchline: Expensive food that immediately spoils
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the contradictions of health food shopping

jokes.text: Well I guess they can go either way.
jokes.setup: Food rotting immediately due to lack of preservatives
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact acceptance of spoiled food
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to the health food consequences

jokes.text: I told you, okay? I'll pay you back when I get my allowance. I mean not this one, but the one after next.
jokes.setup: Andy owing Bart money for spray paint
jokes.punchline: 19-year-old still getting allowance and being broke for multiple weeks
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how pathetic Andy is - adult still on allowance and chronically broke

jokes.text: For you, kid, anything. Anything at all. Although I won't read screen plays. That's for your protection.
jokes.setup: Krusty offering to help Bart with anything
jokes.punchline: Immediately listing extensive limitations
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty characterization showing his selfish generosity

jokes.text: Whoa! Ex-wife! Former writing partner! Bookie! Dog I abandoned! A.A. sponsor! Love child!
jokes.setup: Krusty driving through studio and avoiding people
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire list of people he's wronged
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list that perfectly captures Krusty's history of terrible behavior

jokes.text: I tried, man. But he was like, 'Do this thing!' And I'm like, 'Why, dude?' And he's like, 'I'm your boss!' And I'm like, 'I quit.'
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he quit his job
jokes.punchline: His childish interpretation of basic workplace interaction
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Andy's immaturity and work aversion

jokes.text: An ice cube with a fly in it. If that was in your drink you'd be like, 'I'm not drinking that!'
jokes.setup: Andy showing off his 'achievement' from his failed day at work
jokes.punchline: Presenting something disgusting as if it's clever
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Andy's delusion about what constitutes a good prank

jokes.text: Flush, flush, fish fish, fish, flush
jokes.setup: Educational song about pet lifespans
jokes.punchline: Casual song about flushing dead fish
jokes.explanation: Dark children's programming parody, economical but somewhat cheap shock humor

jokes.text: Don't you know that non-stick pans are made with P.F.O.A.s? / There is only one thing more dangerous than P.F.O.A.s... Plastics made with B.P.A.
jokes.setup: Health-obsessed mother finding fault with Marge's cooking
jokes.punchline: Escalating panic over chemical acronyms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of health food paranoia with realistic chemical anxiety

jokes.text: Seven! Good lord! They've been sucking seven!
jokes.setup: Panic about plastic recycling numbers
jokes.punchline: Treating a recycling number like a death sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with great economy - 'sucking seven' sounds both innocent and horrifying

jokes.text: And I promise you, I will not leave this job unless I flake, or it becomes bogus.
jokes.setup: Andy making a commitment to stick with his job
jokes.punchline: Building in escape clauses that guarantee he'll quit
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Andy's self-aware lack of commitment

jokes.text: Psych! Psych! We have a deal.
jokes.setup: Andy shaking hands on a promise
jokes.punchline: Using outdated 'psych' joke then pretending the fake-out doesn't count
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's juvenile mentality and outdated reference points

jokes.text: You found the precious! / I've eaten your transfats, your red dye number 2
jokes.setup: Marge discovering Homer's junk food stash
jokes.punchline: Homer treating junk food like Gollum's ring, confessing specific ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good Lord of the Rings reference combined with specific junk food addiction confession

jokes.text: Did you know that Lard Glug contains neither lard, nor glug?
jokes.setup: Marge and Homer enjoying junk food together
jokes.punchline: Absurd product name that's completely false advertising
jokes.explanation: Clever fake product name that sounds gross but is meaningless

jokes.text: Did you know that Lard Glug contains neither lard, nor glug?
jokes.setup: Homer sharing junk food with Marge
jokes.punchline: False advertising observation about made-up product
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay about product naming that sounds like real food industry absurdity

jokes.text: Eh, he's about 20 seasons too late
jokes.setup: Someone worried Andy will ruin Krusty's show
jokes.punchline: Meta-reference to The Simpsons' long run and declining quality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware joke about the show's own longevity and quality decline - very brave

jokes.text: I wanted warm water, not worm water!
jokes.setup: Krusty falling into pool during his show
jokes.punchline: Wordplay connecting to the original worm prank
jokes.explanation: Nice callback with wordplay that ties the episode's plot threads together

jokes.text: Looks like I just bought some property on Boner Way
jokes.setup: Andy getting attention from a woman
jokes.punchline: Juvenile euphemism for sexual arousal
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Andy's character but relies on juvenile humor - fits his arrested development

tmdb_overview: Principal Skinner is getting fed up with Bart's pranks, so he informs Bart that he is not the best prankster after all. A former student named Andy Hamilton is hailed as the best prankster, and Bart sets out on a mission to track Andy down. However, when Bart finds out that Andy is a 19-year-old still in his pranking days, they become fast friends.
